Areas were evaluated by micro-CT, histology, and histomorphometric analysis. There is no statistical difference between BG and CBR by evaluating distance ...between the newly formed bone edge and the native bone edge, as well as the bone volume/total volume (BV/TV) ratio at each time point. Micro-CT analysis revealed that the BG area showed slightly higher bone mineral density (BMD) compared to the CBR area, but the difference was not statistically significant. Histological evaluation demonstrated that both BG and CBR areas exhibited new bone formation, with the BG area showing more mature bone with organized osteons, while the cBR area showed active bone remodeling with numerous osteoblasts and osteoclasts. In the defect (D) area, bone formation was observed to a lesser extent compared to BG and CBR areas, with mostly fibrous connective tissue present. Histomorphometric analysis indicated that the bone area fraction was similar in BG and CBR areas, with both being significantly higher than in the D area. These findings suggest that cortical bone

policies_elements_principal.md)\. You cannot use the `NotPrincipal` element in an IAM identity\-based policy\. You can use it in the trust policies for IAM roles and in resource\-based policies\. Resource\-based policies are policies that you embed directly in an IAM resource\. **Important** Very few scenarios require the use of `NotPrincipal`, and we recommend that you explore other authorization options before you decide to use `NotPrincipal`\. ## `NotPrincipal` With `Allow` We strongly recommend that you do not use `NotPrincipal` in the same policy statement as `"Effect": "Allow"`\. Doing so allows all principals *except* the one named in the `NotPrincipal` element\.User: # AWS JSON policy elements: NotPrincipal Use the `NotPrincipal` element to specify the IAM user, federated user, IAM role, AWS account, AWS service, or other principal that is allowed or denied access to a resource. The `NotPrincipal` element enables you to specify an exception to a list of principals. Use this element to deny access to all principals the one named in the `NotPrincipal` element. The syntax for specifying `NotPrincipal` is the same as for specifying [AWS JSON policy elements: Principal](reference_policies_elements_principal.md). You cannot use the `NotPrincipal` element in an IAM identity-based policy. You can use it in the trust policies for IAM roles and in resource-based policies. Resource-based policies are policies that you embed directly in an IAM resource. Important Very few scenarios require the use of `NotPrincipal`, and we recommend that you explore other authorization options before you decide to use `NotPrincipal`. ## `NotPrincipal` With `Allow` We strongly recommend that you do not use `NotPrincipal` in the same policy statement as `"Effect": "Allow"`. Doing so allows all principals the one named in the `NotPrincipal` element. --- System: When using the `NotPrincipal` element, it specifies an exception to a list of principals and is used to deny access to all principals except the one mentioned in the `NotPrincipal` element. This element finds application in trust policies for IAM roles and resource-based policies but not in identity-based policies.
